narrative.headline: 2022 Volkswagen Tiguan: engine leads the owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 236 complaints with NHTSA. The engine is the most reported area at 42%, followed by the electrical system (23%) and the power train (17%). No crashes, fires, or deaths are reported across any area; the only reported injury is a single one tied to the electrical system.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: The single biggest complaint: 98 reports (42%). No crashes, fires, or injuries are reported, but it is the most common area owners raise.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 54 reports (23%), including the only injury in the data (1). No crashes or fires reported.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: 39 reports (17%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries reported. A recurring complaint area worth checking on a test drive.

issues.sample: While driving the vehicle, frequent faults stating "travel assist is currently not available" display on the instrument cluster, along with a warning light. During this time, the travel assist features can not be used. The error occurs randomly, and can happen multiple times back
issues.title: Lane Departure
issues.blurb: 34 reports (14%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Owners often cite the lane-assist features cutting out, so confirm they work.

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: The smallest of the top areas: 28 reports (12%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries reported.
